2898.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Sirin (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'As long as one remains upon the transmitted narrations, he is upon the right path.'
2898.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Sirin (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Selama seseorang tetap berpegang pada riwayat-riwayat yang diriwayatkan, ia berada di jalan yang benar.'

2899.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qilabah (may Allah be pleased with him) that Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Learn knowledge before it is lifted — and its being lifted means that its people depart. Beware — beware of newly invented matters and going deep into disputation, and cling to the ancient way.'
2899.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qilabah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Pelajarilah ilmu sebelum ia diangkat — dan diangkatnya ilmu berarti kepergian para pemiliknya. Hati-hati — hati-hati terhadap perkara-perkara yang baru dibuat-buat dan mendalami perdebatan, dan berpegang teguhlah pada cara lama.'

2900.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qilabah (may Allah be pleased with him) that Ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Hold fast to knowledge before it is lifted — and its being lifted means that its companions are taken away. Hold fast to knowledge, for none of you knows when he will need it or when it will be taken from him. And there will be people who will invite you to knowledge after your scholars have gone — so watch them and look at whom they call to.'
2900.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qilabah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Ibnu Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Berpegang teguhlah pada ilmu sebelum ia diangkat — dan diangkatnya ilmu berarti para sahabatnya dibawa pergi. Berpegang teguhlah pada ilmu, karena tidak ada seorangpun dari kalian yang tahu kapan ia akan membutuhkannya atau kapan ilmu itu akan diambil darinya. Dan akan ada orang-orang yang mengundang kalian kepada ilmu setelah para ulama kalian pergi — maka perhatikanlah mereka dan lihatlah kepada siapa mereka menyeru.'

2901.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sulayman ibn Yasar that a man called Sabigh came to Madinah and began asking about the ambiguous verses of the Quran. Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) sent for him and had date palm fronds prepared. When the man arrived, Umar asked: Who are you? He said: I am the servant of Allah, Sabigh. Umar then struck him with the fronds until blood ran on his head. The man said: Enough, O Commander of the Faithful — that which was in my head has gone. Umar then exiled him to Basra and forbade people from sitting with him until he repented.
2901.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sulaiman ibn Yasar bahwa seorang pria bernama Sabigh datang ke Madinah dan mulai bertanya tentang ayat-ayat samar dari Al-Quran. Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) mengutus utusan kepadanya dan menyiapkan pelepah kurma. Ketika pria itu tiba, Umar bertanya: Siapa kamu? Ia berkata: Aku adalah hamba Allah, Sabigh. Umar kemudian memukulnya dengan pelepah hingga darah mengalir di kepalanya. Pria itu berkata: Cukup, wahai Amirul Mukminin — apa yang ada di kepalaku telah pergi. Umar kemudian mengasingkannya ke Basra dan melarang orang-orang duduk bersamanya hingga ia bertobat.

2902.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ recited: 'It is He who has sent down to you the Book — in it are verses that are precise and others that are ambiguous...' (Quran 3:7). He said: 'When you see those who follow the ambiguous parts of it — those are the ones Allah has described — so beware of them.'
2902.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisha (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ membaca: 'Dialah yang menurunkan Al-Kitab kepadamu — di dalamnya ada ayat-ayat yang muhkamat dan yang lainnya mutasyabihat...' (QS. Ali Imran: 7). Beliau bersabda: 'Ketika kamu melihat orang-orang yang mengikuti bagian-bagian yang samar darinya — merekalah yang Allah gambarkan — maka waspadalah terhadap mereka.'

2903.iwh_en: It was narrated from Shaqiq (may Allah be pleased with him) that Abdullah ibn Mas'ud was asked about something and said: 'I dislike that I make lawful for you something that Allah has forbidden you, or that I forbid what Allah has permitted for you.'
2903.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Shaqiq (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Abdullah ibn Mas'ud ditanya tentang sesuatu dan berkata: 'Aku tidak suka bahwa aku menghalalkan bagi kalian sesuatu yang Allah haramkan atas kalian, atau bahwa aku mengharamkan apa yang Allah halalkan bagi kalian.'

2904.iwh_en: It was narrated from Humayd ibn Abd al-Rahman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'It is more beloved to me to send a questioner away with my inability to answer than to venture beyond my knowledge.'
2904.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Humaid ibn Abd al-Rahman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Lebih aku sukai untuk mengembalikan seorang penanya dengan ketidakmampuanku menjawab daripada memaksakan diri melampaui pengetahuanku.'

2905.iwh_en: It was narrated from Nafi' the freed slave of Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) that Sabigh al-Iraqi began asking about matters of the Quran among the Muslim armies until he reached Egypt. Amr ibn al-As wrote to Umar about him, and Umar wrote back: Send him to me. When he arrived, Umar struck him until he was sufficiently punished, then sent him back. He sent a letter with him to Abu Musa al-Ash'ari: Indeed I have punished this person who was not satisfied with the clear guidance of Allah. So do not sit with him, and instruct the people not to sit with him.
2905.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Nafi' mantan budak Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Sabigh al-Iraqi mulai bertanya tentang perkara-perkara Al-Quran di antara pasukan Muslim hingga ia tiba di Mesir. Amr ibn al-As menulis surat kepada Umar tentangnya, dan Umar membalas: Kirimkan ia kepadaku. Ketika ia tiba, Umar memukulnya hingga ia mendapat hukuman yang cukup, kemudian mengirimnya kembali. Beliau mengirim surat bersamanya kepada Abu Musa al-Asy'ari: Sesungguhnya aku telah menghukum orang ini yang tidak puas dengan petunjuk Allah yang jelas. Maka jangan duduk bersamanya, dan perintahkan orang-orang untuk tidak duduk bersamanya.

2906.iwh_en: It was narrated from Amir al-Sha'bi (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man sought a legal verdict from Ubayy ibn Ka'b and said: 'O Abu al-Mundhir, what do you say about such and such?' He said: 'O my son, has this actually occurred?' He said: No. He said: 'Then leave us until it occurs — if it occurs, we will strive to give you a ruling, Allah willing.'
2906.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Amir al-Sya'bi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang pria meminta fatwa dari Ubay ibn Ka'b dan berkata: 'Wahai Abu al-Mundzir, apa pendapatmu tentang ini dan itu?' Ia berkata: 'Wahai anakku, apakah ini sudah terjadi?' Ia berkata: Tidak. Ia berkata: 'Maka tinggalkan kami sampai itu terjadi — jika terjadi, kami akan berusaha memberikan fatwa, insya Allah.'

2907.iwh_en: It was narrated from Masruq (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was walking with Ubayy ibn Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) when a young man said: What do you say, uncle, about such and such? He said: O my nephew, has this occurred?' He said: No. He said: 'Then excuse us until it occurs. If it occurs we will give you a ruling about it, Allah willing.'
2907.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Masruq (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku sedang berjalan bersama Ubay ibn Ka'b (radhiyallahu 'anhu) ketika seorang pemuda berkata: Apa pendapatmu, paman, tentang ini dan itu? Ia berkata: Wahai keponakanku, apakah ini sudah terjadi?' Ia berkata: Tidak. Ia berkata: 'Maka maafkan kami sampai itu terjadi. Jika terjadi kami akan memberikan fatwa tentangnya, insya Allah.'

2908.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-A'mash (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'When Ibrahim al-Nakha'i was asked about something, he would only give the answer to exactly what was asked — nothing more.'
2908.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-A'masy (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika Ibrahim al-Nakha'i ditanya tentang sesuatu, ia hanya memberikan jawaban tepat untuk apa yang ditanyakan — tidak lebih.'

2909.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muhammad ibn Sirin (may Allah be pleased with him) that he would not give rulings on matters of marriage and divorce if there was scholarly disagreement about them.
2909.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muhammad ibn Sirin (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia tidak mau memberikan fatwa tentang masalah pernikahan dan perceraian jika ada perbedaan pendapat ulama tentangnya.

2910.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Salt ibn Rashid (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I asked Tawus about a matter and he said: Has this occurred?' I said: Yes. He said: By Allah? I said: By Allah. He then said: Our companions informed us from Mu'adh ibn Jabal — and he proceeded to give the ruling based on what was narrated.'
2910.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Salt ibn Rasyid (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bertanya kepada Thawus tentang suatu perkara dan ia berkata: Apakah ini sudah terjadi?' Aku berkata: Ya. Ia berkata: Demi Allah? Aku berkata: Demi Allah. Ia kemudian berkata: Para sahabat kami memberitahukan kami dari Mu'adz ibn Jabal — dan ia melanjutkan untuk memberikan fatwa berdasarkan apa yang diriwayatkan.'

2911.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he was asked about a man who experienced two Ramadans — whether he must make up for the days he missed in the first one before the second one arrives. He said: Has this occurred or not? They said: It has not occurred yet. He said: Then leave it until it occurs.
2911.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia ditanya tentang seorang pria yang mengalami dua Ramadan — apakah ia harus mengganti hari-hari yang ia lewatkan pada yang pertama sebelum yang kedua tiba. Ia berkata: Apakah ini sudah terjadi atau belum? Mereka berkata: Belum terjadi. Ia berkata: Maka tinggalkanlah sampai terjadi.

2912.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubayd ibn Jurayj (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I used to sit with Ibn Umar one day and with Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) another day in Makkah. What Ibn Umar said about what he was asked — I would hear it from Ibn Abbas. And what Ibn Abbas said — I would hear it from Ibn Umar. Each would say: What I know I speak, what I do not know I keep silent about.'
2912.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubaid ibn Juraij (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku biasa duduk bersama Ibnu Umar satu hari dan bersama Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) hari berikutnya di Makkah. Apa yang Ibnu Umar katakan tentang apa yang ia ditanya — aku mendengarnya dari Ibnu Abbas. Dan apa yang Ibnu Abbas katakan — aku mendengarnya dari Ibnu Umar. Masing-masing berkata: Apa yang aku ketahui aku ucapkan, apa yang tidak aku ketahui aku diamkan.'

2913.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Wa'il (may Allah be pleased with him) that Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Learn — for none of you knows when he will be turned to for guidance.'
2913.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Wa'il (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Belajarlah — karena tidak ada seorangpun dari kalian yang tahu kapan ia akan dituju untuk mendapatkan bimbingan.'

2914.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubaydullah ibn Abi Ja'far (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The most daring of you in giving religious verdicts is the most daring of you toward the Fire.'
2914.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubaidullah ibn Abi Ja'far (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Yang paling berani di antara kalian dalam memberikan fatwa adalah yang paling berani di antara kalian terhadap neraka.'

2915.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'Whoever introduces a new opinion not found in the Book of Allah and for which no Sunnah from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ has precedent — he does not know what awaits him when he meets Allah, the Mighty and Majestic.'
2915.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Siapapun yang memperkenalkan pendapat baru yang tidak ada dalam Kitabullah dan tidak ada Sunnah dari Rasulullah ﷺ yang mendahuluinya — ia tidak tahu apa yang menunggunya ketika ia bertemu Allah, Yang Maha Perkasa dan Maha Agung.'

2916.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: 'Whoever is given a religious verdict without knowledge, his sin is upon the one who gave the verdict.'
2916.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Siapapun yang diberi fatwa tanpa ilmu, dosanya ada pada orang yang memberikan fatwa itu.'

2917.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'Whoever gives a religious verdict based on which the questioner is led into confusion — his sin is upon him.'
2917.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Siapapun yang memberikan fatwa yang menyebabkan penanya tersesat — dosanya ada padanya.'

2918.iwh_en: It was narrated from Maymun ibn Mihran (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'When a dispute was brought to Abu Bakr (may Allah be pleased with him), he would look in the Book of Allah. If he found in it what would settle their matter, he would decide by it. If he did not find it in the Book of Allah, and he knew a Sunnah of the Prophet ﷺ, he would decide by it. If not, he would ask the people: Do you know of a Sunnah of the Prophet ﷺ in this matter? If they told him, he would decide by it. If not, he would gather the leading Muslims and consult them. If they agreed on a matter, he would decide by it.'
2918.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Maimun ibn Mihran (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika suatu perselisihan dibawa kepada Abu Bakr (radhiyallahu 'anhu), ia akan mencari dalam Kitabullah. Jika ia menemukan di dalamnya apa yang akan menyelesaikan perkara mereka, ia akan memutuskan berdasarkan itu. Jika ia tidak menemukan dalam Kitabullah, dan ia mengetahui Sunnah Nabi ﷺ, ia akan memutuskan berdasarkan itu. Jika tidak, ia akan bertanya kepada orang-orang: Apakah kalian mengetahui Sunnah Nabi ﷺ dalam perkara ini? Jika mereka memberitahunya, ia akan memutuskan berdasarkan itu. Jika tidak, ia akan mengumpulkan para pemimpin Muslim dan berkonsultasi dengan mereka. Jika mereka sepakat tentang suatu perkara, ia akan memutuskan berdasarkan itu.'

2919.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Suhayl (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'My wife had a vow to perform i'tikaf for three days in the Sacred Mosque. I asked Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, in whose presence Ibn Shihab was. He said: Ask Ibn Shihab. Ibn Shihab said: There is no i'tikaf except in the three mosques — the Sacred Mosque, the Prophet's Mosque in Madinah, and the mosque in Jerusalem. So she should perform it in the mosque of the Prophet ﷺ in Madinah.'
2919.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Suhayl (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Istriku memiliki nazar untuk beri'tikaf selama tiga hari di Masjidil Haram. Aku bertanya kepada Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, yang di hadapannya ada Ibnu Syihab. Ia berkata: Tanyakan kepada Ibnu Syihab. Ibnu Syihab berkata: Tidak ada i'tikaf kecuali di tiga masjid — Masjidil Haram, Masjid Nabi di Madinah, dan masjid di Yerusalem. Maka hendaklah ia melaksanakannya di masjid Nabi ﷺ di Madinah.'

2920.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Nadrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that when Abu Salamah came to Basra, he and al-Hasan came to him. Abu Salamah said to al-Hasan: Are you al-Hasan? No one in Basra was more beloved to me than you — because of what I heard about you and your opinions in religious rulings. But I heard you give a ruling that surprised me: that a man must perform ghusl for every prayer during his entire life just because of a woman with irregular bleeding. Where does that come from? Al-Hasan said: From Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her).
2920.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Nadrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika Abu Salamah datang ke Basra, ia dan al-Hasan datang menemuinya. Abu Salamah berkata kepada al-Hasan: Apakah kamu al-Hasan? Tidak ada di Basra yang lebih aku sukai daripada kamu — karena apa yang aku dengar tentang kamu dan pendapatmu dalam fatwa agama. Tetapi aku mendengar kamu memberi fatwa yang mengejutkan aku: bahwa seorang pria harus mandi untuk setiap shalat selama hidupnya hanya karena seorang wanita dengan perdarahan tidak teratur. Dari mana itu? Al-Hasan berkata: Dari Aisha (radhiyallahu 'anha).

2921.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) met him during tawaf and said to him: 'O Abu al-Sha'tha', you are one of the jurists of Basra — so do not give rulings except based on what is transmitted from the Quran or Sunnah. If you do otherwise, you will perish and cause others to perish.'
2921.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Zaid (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Ibnu Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bertemu dengannya saat tawaf dan berkata kepadanya: 'Wahai Abu al-Sya'tsa', kamu adalah salah satu ahli fiqh Basra — maka jangan memberikan fatwa kecuali berdasarkan apa yang diriwayatkan dari Al-Quran atau Sunnah. Jika kamu berbuat selain itu, kamu akan binasa dan menyebabkan orang lain binasa.'

2922.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'There will come a time when we will not be asked to give rulings and we will not be fit for it — and Allah has decreed that that time will come. So from now on, whoever among you is asked for a religious verdict, let him give the verdict of what he knows, and let him say "Allah knows best" about what he does not know — for indeed saying "Allah knows best" about something is itself a form of knowledge.'
2922.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Akan datang suatu masa ketika kami tidak akan diminta memberikan fatwa dan kami tidak akan layak untuk itu — dan Allah telah menetapkan bahwa masa itu akan datang. Maka mulai sekarang, siapapun di antara kalian yang diminta fatwa agama, hendaklah ia memberikan fatwa tentang apa yang ia ketahui, dan hendaklah ia mengatakan "Allah lebih mengetahui" tentang apa yang tidak ia ketahui — karena sesungguhnya mengatakan "Allah lebih mengetahui" tentang sesuatu itu sendiri adalah suatu bentuk ilmu.'

2923.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that when he was asked about a matter: if it was in the Quran, he would inform based on it. If it was not in the Quran but was from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he would inform based on it. If it was not in the Quran nor from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ but was from Abu Bakr and Umar, he would inform based on it. If it was none of the above, he would give his own considered opinion.
2923.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ketika ia ditanya tentang suatu perkara: jika itu ada dalam Al-Quran, ia akan memberitahu berdasarkan itu. Jika tidak ada dalam Al-Quran tetapi berasal dari Rasulullah ﷺ, ia akan memberitahu berdasarkan itu. Jika tidak ada dalam Al-Quran dan tidak dari Rasulullah ﷺ tetapi dari Abu Bakr dan Umar, ia akan memberitahu berdasarkan itu. Jika tidak ada satupun di atas, ia akan memberikan pendapat pribadinya yang telah dipertimbangkan.

2924.iwh_en: It was narrated from Shurayh (may Allah be pleased with him) that Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) wrote to him: 'If something comes to you that is in the Book of Allah, give judgment according to it and do not let men distract you from it. If something comes to you that is not in the Book of Allah, follow the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. If something comes to you that is neither in the Book of Allah nor in the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, choose between giving your own judgment or delaying. And I think delaying is better for you.'
2924.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Syuraih (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Umar ibn al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) menulis kepadanya: 'Jika sesuatu datang kepadamu yang ada dalam Kitabullah, berikan keputusan berdasarkan itu dan jangan biarkan orang-orang mengalihkanmu darinya. Jika sesuatu datang kepadamu yang tidak ada dalam Kitabullah, ikutilah Sunnah Rasulullah ﷺ. Jika sesuatu datang kepadamu yang tidak ada dalam Kitabullah maupun Sunnah Rasulullah ﷺ, pilihlah antara memberikan keputusanmu sendiri atau menunda. Dan aku pikir menunda lebih baik bagimu.'

2925.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) through people from Hims among the companions of Mu'adh that the Prophet ﷺ sent Mu'adh to Yemen. He said: How will you judge when a matter is brought before you? He said: I will judge by the Book of Allah. He said: And if you do not find it in the Book of Allah? He said: Then by the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He said: And if you do not find it in the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah? He said: Then I will strive to give my own considered judgment and not fall short. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ struck his chest and said: Praise be to Allah who has guided the messenger of the Messenger of Allah to that which pleases the Messenger of Allah.
2925.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adz ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui orang-orang dari Hims di antara para sahabat Mu'adz bahwa Nabi ﷺ mengutus Mu'adz ke Yaman. Beliau berkata: Bagaimana kamu akan memutuskan ketika suatu perkara diajukan kepadamu? Ia berkata: Aku akan memutuskan berdasarkan Kitabullah. Beliau berkata: Dan jika kamu tidak menemukannya dalam Kitabullah? Ia berkata: Maka berdasarkan Sunnah Rasulullah ﷺ. Beliau berkata: Dan jika kamu tidak menemukannya dalam Sunnah Rasulullah? Ia berkata: Maka aku akan berijtihad dengan pendapatku yang dipertimbangkan dan tidak akan kurang. Rasulullah ﷺ menepuk dadanya dan berkata: Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah membimbing utusan Rasulullah kepada apa yang memuaskan Rasulullah.

2926.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'There has come upon us a time when we are not asked and we are not at that level. But Allah has decreed that matters will come — and when they come, anyone among you who is asked must give a ruling based on what he knows.'
2926.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Telah datang atas kami suatu masa ketika kami tidak ditanya dan kami tidak pada tingkat itu. Tetapi Allah telah menetapkan bahwa perkara-perkara akan datang — dan ketika mereka datang, siapapun di antara kalian yang ditanya harus memberikan fatwa berdasarkan apa yang ia ketahui.'

2927.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'O people — you will introduce innovations and innovations will be introduced for you. When you see a newly invented matter, hold fast to the original way.'
2927.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Wahai manusia — kalian akan memperkenalkan bid'ah dan bid'ah akan diperkenalkan untuk kalian. Ketika kalian melihat perkara yang baru dibuat-buat, berpegang teguhlah pada cara semula.'

2928.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muhammad (may Allah be pleased with him) that Umar said to Ibn Mas'ud: 'Have I not been informed that you give religious verdicts when you are not an appointed authority? Let the one who enjoys the cold of something bear its heat — meaning: let those who enjoyed prestige bear the responsibilities it brings.'
2928.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muhammad (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Umar berkata kepada Ibnu Mas'ud: 'Bukankah aku telah diberitahu bahwa kamu memberikan fatwa padahal kamu bukan pejabat yang ditunjuk? Biarkan orang yang menikmati kesejukan sesuatu menanggung panasnya — artinya: biarkan mereka yang menikmati prestise menanggung tanggung jawab yang dibawanya.'

2929.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The one who gives religious verdicts on every matter he is asked about is indeed a fool.'
2929.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Orang yang memberikan fatwa agama atas setiap perkara yang ia ditanyakan adalah seorang yang bodoh.'

2930.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Only three types of people should give religious verdicts: an imam or ruler, a man who knows the abrogating verses of the Quran from the abrogated ones.' They said: And who is that, O Hudhayfah? He said: 'Umar ibn al-Khattab, and a man who is compelled by necessity.'
2930.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Hanya tiga jenis orang yang boleh memberikan fatwa agama: seorang imam atau penguasa, seorang pria yang mengetahui ayat-ayat Al-Quran yang menghapus dari yang dihapus.' Mereka berkata: Dan siapakah itu, wahai Hudzaifah? Ia berkata: 'Umar ibn al-Khattab, dan seseorang yang dipaksa oleh keadaan darurat.'

2931.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Only one of three people should give religious verdicts: a man who knows the abrogating verses of the Quran from the abrogated, a ruler who has no choice but to give verdicts, or a fool who makes himself their equal.'
2931.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Hanya salah satu dari tiga orang yang boleh memberikan fatwa agama: seorang pria yang mengetahui ayat-ayat Al-Quran yang menghapus dari yang dihapus, seorang penguasa yang tidak punya pilihan selain memberikan fatwa, atau seorang yang bodoh yang menjadikan dirinya setara dengan mereka.'

2932.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Whoever among you knows something, let him say it. And whoever does not know, let him say about what he does not know: Allah knows best — for indeed when a scholar is asked about what he does not know and says "Allah knows best", that is half of knowledge.'
2932.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Siapapun di antara kalian yang mengetahui sesuatu, hendaklah ia mengatakannya. Dan siapapun yang tidak tahu, hendaklah ia mengatakan tentang apa yang tidak ia ketahui: Allah lebih mengetahui — karena sesungguhnya ketika seorang ulama ditanya tentang apa yang tidak ia ketahui dan berkata "Allah lebih mengetahui", itu adalah setengah dari ilmu.'

2933.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Musa al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said in a sermon: 'Whoever has knowledge, let him teach it to the people. And let him beware of saying what he has no knowledge of — lest he depart from religion and become a liar.'
2933.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Musa al-Asy'ari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata dalam sebuah khutbah: 'Siapapun yang memiliki ilmu, hendaklah ia mengajarkannya kepada orang-orang. Dan hendaklah ia waspada terhadap mengatakan apa yang tidak ia ketahui — agar ia tidak keluar dari agama dan menjadi pendusta.'

2934.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with him and his family) that he said: 'How cooling it is to the liver — when I am asked about something I do not know — to say: Allah knows best.'
2934.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ali ibn Abi Thalib (radhiyallahu 'anhu wa alihi) bahwa ia berkata: 'Betapa menyejukkannya bagi hati — ketika aku ditanya tentang sesuatu yang tidak aku ketahui — untuk mengatakan: Allah lebih mengetahui.'

2935.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with him and his family) that he said: 'O how cooling it is to the liver — to say about what you do not know: Allah knows best.'
2935.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ali ibn Abi Thalib (radhiyallahu 'anhu wa alihi) bahwa ia berkata: 'Oh betapa menyejukkannya bagi hati — untuk mengatakan tentang apa yang tidak kamu ketahui: Allah lebih mengetahui.'

2936.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with him and his family) that he said: 'When you are asked about something you do not know, flee.' They said: And how does one flee, O Commander of the Faithful? He said: 'Say: Allah knows best.'
2936.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ali ibn Abi Thalib (radhiyallahu 'anhu wa alihi) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika kalian ditanya tentang sesuatu yang tidak kalian ketahui, larilah.' Mereka berkata: Dan bagaimana seseorang berlari, wahai Amirul Mukminin? Ia berkata: 'Katakanlah: Allah lebih mengetahui.'

2937.iwh_en: It was narrated from Azrah al-Tamimi (may Allah be pleased with him) that Ali (may Allah be pleased with him and his family) said: 'How cooling it is to the liver — three times.' They said: What is that, O Commander of the Faithful? He said: 'That a man is asked about something he does not know and says: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, knows best.'
2937.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Azrah al-Tamimi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu wa alihi) berkata: 'Betapa menyejukkannya bagi hati — tiga kali.' Mereka berkata: Apa itu, wahai Amirul Mukminin? Ia berkata: 'Bahwa seseorang ditanya tentang sesuatu yang tidak ia ketahui dan berkata: Allah, Yang Maha Perkasa dan Maha Agung, lebih mengetahui.'

2938.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that a man asked him about a matter and he said: I have no knowledge of it. When the man turned to leave, Ibn Umar said: 'How good is what Ibn Umar said! He was asked about what he does not know and said: I have no knowledge of it.'
2938.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa seorang pria bertanya kepadanya tentang suatu perkara dan ia berkata: Aku tidak memiliki pengetahuan tentangnya. Ketika pria itu berbalik hendak pergi, Ibnu Umar berkata: 'Alangkah baiknya apa yang dikatakan Ibnu Umar! Ia ditanya tentang apa yang tidak ia ketahui dan berkata: Aku tidak memiliki pengetahuan tentangnya.'

2939.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Sha'bi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Saying "I do not know" is half of knowledge.'
2939.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Sya'bi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Mengatakan "Aku tidak tahu" adalah setengah dari ilmu.'

2940.iwh_en: It was narrated from Nafi' (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came to Ibn Umar and asked him about something. He said: I have no knowledge of it. After the man left, Ibn Umar turned and said: 'How good is what Ibn Umar said! He was asked about what he does not know and said: I have no knowledge of it.'
2940.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Nafi' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang pria datang kepada Ibnu Umar dan bertanya kepadanya tentang sesuatu. Ia berkata: Aku tidak memiliki pengetahuan tentangnya. Setelah pria itu pergi, Ibnu Umar berbalik dan berkata: 'Alangkah baiknya apa yang dikatakan Ibnu Umar! Ia ditanya tentang apa yang tidak ia ketahui dan berkata: Aku tidak memiliki pengetahuan tentangnya.'

2941.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mughirah (may Allah be pleased with him) that Amir al-Sha'bi, when asked about something, would say: I do not know. If they pressed him, he would say: If you wish, I swear by Allah — I have no knowledge of this.
2941.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mughirah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Amir al-Sya'bi, ketika ditanya tentang sesuatu, akan berkata: Aku tidak tahu. Jika mereka mendesaknya, ia akan berkata: Jika kamu mau, aku bersumpah demi Allah — aku tidak memiliki pengetahuan tentang ini.

2942.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Sirin (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I do not mind whether I am asked about what I know or what I do not know — for when I am asked about what I know, I say what I know. And when I am asked about what I do not know, I say: I do not know.'
2942.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Sirin (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku tidak keberatan apakah aku ditanya tentang apa yang aku ketahui atau apa yang tidak aku ketahui — karena ketika aku ditanya tentang apa yang aku ketahui, aku mengatakan apa yang aku ketahui. Dan ketika aku ditanya tentang apa yang tidak aku ketahui, aku berkata: Aku tidak tahu.'

2943.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-A'mash (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I never heard Ibrahim al-Nakha'i say ever: this is lawful or this is forbidden. He would only say: they used to dislike this, or they used to prefer this.'
2943.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-A'masy (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku tidak pernah mendengar Ibrahim al-Nakha'i berkata pernah: ini halal atau ini haram. Ia hanya berkata: mereka biasa tidak menyukai ini, atau mereka biasa lebih menyukai ini.'

2944.iwh_en: It was narrated from Shaqiq (may Allah be pleased with him) that Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'What will you do when a trial (fitnah) covers you — in which the old man grows old and the young man is raised in it, and people take it as the Sunnah? If any part of it changes, they say: The Sunnah has been changed.' They said: When will that be, O Ibn Mas'ud? He said: 'When your scholars are few and your reciters are many, and understanding is scarce and learning is abundant, and when leadership is in the hands of a few.'
2944.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Shaqiq (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Bagaimana keadaan kalian ketika fitnah menyelimuti kalian — di mana orang tua menjadi tua dan anak muda dibesarkan di dalamnya, dan orang-orang menjadikannya sebagai Sunnah? Jika ada bagian darinya yang berubah, mereka berkata: Sunnah telah berubah.' Mereka berkata: Kapan itu akan terjadi, wahai Ibnu Mas'ud? Ia berkata: 'Ketika ulama kalian sedikit dan para pembaca (Quran) kalian banyak, dan pemahaman langka sementara belajar melimpah, dan ketika kepemimpinan berada di tangan segelintir orang.'

2945.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'What will you do when a trial covers you — in which the old man grows old and the young man is raised in it? When any part of it is left, people say: a Sunnah has been abandoned. When the people's scholars and jurists go, they will not be replaced.'
2945.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Bagaimana keadaan kalian ketika fitnah menyelimuti kalian — di mana orang tua menjadi tua dan anak muda dibesarkan di dalamnya? Ketika ada bagian darinya yang ditinggalkan, orang-orang berkata: satu Sunnah telah ditinggalkan. Ketika para ulama dan ahli fiqh pergi, mereka tidak akan digantikan.'

2946.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Awzai (may Allah be pleased with him) that it used to be said: 'Woe to those who study religion for other than worship, and who make the forbidden things lawful through doubtful arguments.'
2946.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Awza'i (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa biasa dikatakan: 'Celakalah orang-orang yang belajar agama bukan untuk ibadah, dan yang menghalalkan perkara-perkara yang diharamkan melalui argumen-argumen yang meragukan.'

2947.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'There will not come upon you a year except that it is worse than the year that preceded it — but I do not mean the abundance of provisions or wealth, but rather the departure of your scholars and righteous ones. Then there will come people who give their own opinions and destroy religion.'
2947.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Tidak akan datang atas kalian suatu tahun kecuali itu lebih buruk dari tahun yang mendahuluinya — tetapi aku tidak maksudkan kelimpahan rezeki atau kekayaan, melainkan kepergian para ulama dan orang-orang saleh kalian. Kemudian akan datang orang-orang yang memberikan pendapat mereka sendiri dan menghancurkan agama.'
